name: Lerín, Allo, and Lodosa Loop
name_original: Andosilla - Allo – vuelta por Lerín y Lodosa
description: Moderate 34.4-mile road cycling route in Navarre, passing through Andosilla, Allo, Lerín, and Lodosa with scenic views.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Andosilla are situated in the Foral Community of Navarra, Spain, offering diverse landscapes for cyclists. The municipality lies at an altitude of 332 meters on the banks of the Ebro River, providing varied terrain. Cyclists can expect routes that traverse river valleys, hillsides, and the broader scenic areas of Navarra. The region's natural features include long rivers, streams, and a mix of agricultural lands and more rugged areas. Best road cycling routes around Andosilla The most popular road cycling route is Lerín, Allo, and Lodosa Loop, a 34.4 miles (55.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 25 minutes to complete, offering a moderate ride through local towns and countryside. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Lodosa – Lodosa loop from Cárcar, a moderate 43.1 miles (69.4 km) path. This route explores the surroundings of Cárcar, featuring varied terrain suitable for a longer ride. Local road cyclists also love the Lodosa – Views of Lerín loop from Andosilla, a 69.2 miles (111.4 km) trail leading through the wider region with views towards Lerín, often completed in about 4 hours 53 minutes. Road cycling around Andosilla is defined by routes along riverbanks, through hillsides, and across the diverse landscapes of Navarra. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more difficult, longer rides. The routes in Andosilla are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from a few reviews. More than 500 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Andosilla's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Andosilla, and what are their difficulty levels?
A: There are over 40 road cycling routes around Andosilla. You'll find options for various skill levels, including 5 easy routes, 20 moderate routes, and 15 more challenging routes for experienced cyclists.

Q: What kind of landscapes and natural features can I expect to see while road cycling near Andosilla?
A: Road cycling around Andosilla offers a diverse range of landscapes. You'll traverse river valleys along the Ebro River, cycle through hillsides, and experience the broader scenic areas of Navarra. The region features long rivers, streams, and a mix of agricultural lands and more rugged terrain. You might also encounter picturesque woodlands and, in the wider Navarra region, even impressive mountain ranges and unique karstic landscapes.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or attractions along the road cycling routes in Andosilla?
A: Yes, several points of interest can be found near the cycling routes. For instance, you could visit &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5982899" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Perdiguero Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, explore the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5259467" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calahorra Old Town&lt;/a&gt;, or pass through the charming settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5692290" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Adrián&lt;/a&gt;. For scenic views, consider stopping at &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5237472" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de la Peña&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about the routes in Andosilla?
A: The road cycling routes around Andosilla are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, which includes routes along riverbanks and through hillsides, offering a good mix for different preferences. The routes provide a great way to explore the diverse landscapes of Navarra.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available around Andosilla?
A: Yes, many of the routes around Andosilla are designed as loops, offering convenient circular journeys. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e985080766/lerin-allo-and-lodosa-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lerín, Allo, and Lodosa Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate ride, and the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20151236" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lodosa – Views of Lerín loop from Andosilla&lt;/a&gt; also offers a circular experience.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Andosilla?
A: The region generally offers good cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are often ideal, with milder temperatures and pleasant scenery. Summers can be warm, especially in the river valleys, while winters are cooler but still offer opportunities for cycling, though some higher elevation routes might be less accessible.

Q: Are there any road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Andosilla?
A: While many routes are moderate to difficult, there are easier options available. The region's greenways, like the Plazaola Greenway in the wider Navarra area, often utilize old railway lines, providing flat, safe, and scenic paths ideal for families or those seeking a more relaxed ride. Look for routes categorized as 'easy' in the komoot guide for suitable options.

Q: Where can I find parking for my car when starting a road cycling route in Andosilla?
A: Andosilla, being a municipality, typically offers various parking options within the town itself. For specific route starting points, it's advisable to check the individual route details on komoot, as many routes begin from accessible locations with nearby parking facilities. Larger towns like Lodosa or Cárcar, which are often part of the loops, also provide parking.

Q: Can I reach Andosilla and its cycling routes using public transport with my bike?
A: Public transport options to Andosilla, such as bus services, may be available. However, policies regarding bicycles on public transport can vary. It's recommended to check with local bus operators in advance for their specific rules on carrying bikes, especially for longer journeys or during peak times.

Q: Are there any cafes or pubs along the road cycling routes in Andosilla where I can stop for a break?
A: Many road cycling routes in the Andosilla region pass through or near small towns and villages like Lerín, Allo, Lodosa, and Cárcar. These settlements typically offer local cafes, bars, or restaurants where you can stop for refreshments, a meal, or a quick coffee break. It's a great way to experience local culture and refuel during your ride.

Q: What is the longest road cycling route available around Andosilla?
A: One of the longer and more challenging routes is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20151236" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lodosa – Views of Lerín loop from Andosilla&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 69.2 miles (111.4 km) and typically takes around 4 hours and 53 minutes to complete. Another significant option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38230704" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gollizo Pool (Jubera River) – Ventas Blancas loop from Lodosa&lt;/a&gt;, covering 102.4 km.

Q: Are there any road cycling routes that offer significant elevation gain for a challenging ride?
A: Yes, for cyclists seeking a challenge, routes around Andosilla offer substantial elevation gain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20151236" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lodosa – Views of Lerín loop from Andosilla&lt;/a&gt;, for example, features over 1000 meters of ascent. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38230704" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gollizo Pool (Jubera River) – Ventas Blancas loop from Lodosa&lt;/a&gt;, which includes over 1200 meters of climbing.
tips: Navarrese border town located on the banks of the Ebro River, one of the most important in Spain. The area around the town is known for its famous piquillo peppers. If you pass by here, don't forget to taste them.
Beautiful views of the small but picturesque town of Lerín surrounded by a very typical landscape of the Navarrese interior.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The Andosilla - Allo – return via Lerín and Lodosa route is approximately 55.4 km (34.4 miles) long and typically takes around 2 hours and 25 minutes to complete by racebike. This duration can vary based on your cycling speed and any stops you make along the way.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain and scenery can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: This road cycling route in Navarre, Spain, features varied landscapes. You can expect to encounter gentle, rolling hills and irrigated cropland typical of the region. While cycling, you'll pass through local towns and the countryside, with potential views of the wider Navarre area known for its picturesque natural features.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The route is classified as 'moderate'. This means it's suitable for cyclists with a reasonable level of fitness and some experience. While not overly demanding for seasoned riders, beginners might find some sections challenging. It's not specifically designed as a beginner-friendly route.

faqs.question: Are there any notable towns or viewpoints along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes through or near several interesting points. You'll cycle through the town of Lodosa, and you can enjoy Views of Lerín, offering scenic perspectives of the area.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the route?
faqs.answer: Specific parking information for the starting point in Andosilla is not detailed in the provided data. However, as a road cycling route connecting several towns, you can typically find public parking options within Andosilla or other towns along the route like Allo, Lerín, or Lodosa. It's advisable to check local parking regulations upon arrival.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Navarre generally offers good cycling conditions for much of the year. To enjoy the most pleasant weather, consider cycling during the spring (April-June) or autumn (September-October) when temperatures are milder and the scenery is vibrant. Summer can be warm, while winter might bring cooler temperatures and a higher chance of rain.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route or the Yesos de la Ribera Estellesa park?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this road route. Road cycling routes in Navarre, including this one, are generally open to the public. The route is located in the region of Yesos de la Ribera Estellesa, but there are no specific fees or permits mentioned for cycling through this area.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As a road cycling route, dogs are generally not recommended to accompany cyclists due to safety concerns with traffic and the pace of cycling. If you wish to bring a dog, it would need to be in a suitable trailer or carrier, but the route itself is not designed with dog-walking or off-leash activities in mind.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route connects several towns like Andosilla, Allo, Lerín, and Lodosa, you will find various opportunities for refreshments. These towns typically offer cafes, restaurants, and shops where you can stop for food, drinks, or to refill water bottles.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other official cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route briefly intersects with a few other official paths. Approximately 1% of the route overlaps with the 'Sendero Histórico: Navarra', 7% with 'Los Pinos', and a very small section (less than 1%) with 'Paseo de la Paticuesta'.

faqs.question: Is this a loop route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route name 'Andosilla - Allo – return via Lerín and Lodosa' indicates that it is a loop route, starting and ending in Andosilla.
